content.text:
Here’s what was going on and what we changed.

### What you removed in the preview
The whole **`header.woocommerce-products-header`** block (including the “Shop” **`h1`**) under **`main#main.site-main`**.

### What was in source
The child theme already hid the title via filters/CSS and used a custom **`woocommerce/loop/header.php`**, but that file still wrapped **`woocommerce_archive_description`** in:

`<header class="woocommerce-products-header woocommerce-products-header--no-title">…</header>`.

### What we did
**Updated** [`printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/woocommerce/loop/header.php`](printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/woocommerce/loop/header.php) so it **no longer outputs any `<header>`**. It only runs **`do_action('woocommerce_archive_description')`**, so shop/category intro text from WooCommerce still appears when present, without that header element.

Existing **`style.css`** rules for `.woocommerce-products-header` are unchanged; they still help if another plugin prints that markup. They simply won’t apply to this template anymore.

If you also want archive descriptions removed from this spot (empty `main` until sorting), say so and we can strip or relocate that hook.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Applying hook removals so the breadcrumb and archive header aren't output, adding a replacement hook for archive descriptions, hiding any leftover `.storefront-breadcrumb` on PLP via CSS, and removing the redundant `loop/header.php` override.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### 1. **Storefront breadcrumb (`div.storefront-breadcrumb` → Home / Shop)**  
Storefront doesn’t use a `storefront_breadcrumb` callback; it attaches **`woocommerce_breadcrumb`** to **`storefront_before_content`** (priority 10). On shop and product taxonomy archives we now **`remove_action('storefront_before_content', 'woocommerce_breadcrumb', 10)`** so that strip isn’t rendered.

CSS was also updated so any leftover `.storefront-breadcrumb` on PLP (including **`body.woocommerce-shop`**) is **`display: none !important`**, instead of only tweaking margins.

### 2. **`header.woocommerce-products-header` (“Shop”)**  
That markup comes from WooCommerce’s default **`woocommerce_product_taxonomy_archive_header`** on **`woocommerce_shop_loop_header`**, which loads **`loop/header.php`** (with the `<header class="woocommerce-products-header">` wrapper).  

We now **`remove_action('woocommerce_shop_loop_header', 'woocommerce_product_taxonomy_archive_header', 10)`** on shop / product taxonomies, and add a **`woocommerce_shop_loop_header`** callback at **priority 5** that only runs **`do_action('woocommerce_archive_description')`**, so category/shop intro copy can still show **without** the products header block.

### 3. **Removed `woocommerce/loop/header.php`**  
With the hook-based approach, that override was redundant; deleting it avoids two competing behaviors.

---

**Files touched**

- [`printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/functions.php`](printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/functions.php) — breadcrumb + archive header removal and archive-description hook  
- [`printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/style.css`](printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/style.css) — hide PLP `.storefront-breadcrumb`  
- **Deleted:** `printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/woocommerce/loop/header.php`

Breadcrumbs on **single product** (and other non-PLP routes) are unchanged. If you want breadcrumbs gone everywhere WooCommerce runs, say so and we can widen the conditions.

[REDACTED]

Tracing why the breadcrumb or products header still appears: checking installed WooCommerce/Storefront hooks and whether our removals match.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s **why it could still show** even after the earlier PHP changes:

### 1. **Block catalog vs classic PHP**
WooCommerce’s **blockified** Product Catalog (`archive-product.html`) adds:

- `<!-- wp:woocommerce/breadcrumbs /-->` → markup like `wc-block-breadcrumbs` / `woocommerce-breadcrumb`
- `<!-- wp:query-title {"type":"archive"} /-->` → the big **“Shop”** title  

Those are rendered with **`render_block`**. They **do not** use Storefront’s `storefront_before_content` hook or `woocommerce_shop_loop_header`, so `remove_action(...)` never touched them. Your DOM could be classic (`storefront-breadcrumb` + `woocommerce-products-header`) **or** block (`wc-block-breadcrumbs` + query title), depending on settings.

### 2. **Hook timing**
Running removals only on `wp` can be flaky for **main-query** checks. Removals are now on **`template_redirect` (priority 998)** so `is_shop()` / taxonomy / archive state matches what WordPress will render.

### What we changed just now

- **`printonet_theme_is_product_listing()`** – centralizes PLP detection (`is_shop()`, `is_product_taxonomy()`, `is_post_type_archive('product')`).
- **`template_redirect`** – same removals as before, plus **`woocommerce_before_main_content`** breadcrumb at several priorities (covers Storefront/WC mismatch).
- **`render_block` (priority 999)** – on PLP, strips **`woocommerce/breadcrumbs`** and **`core/query-title`** when `type === 'archive'`.
- **`style.css`** – also hides **`.wc-block-breadcrumbs`**, **`nav.woocommerce-breadcrumb`**, and the full **`.woocommerce-products-header`** on PLP as a fallback (cache / plugins).

After deploy, do a **hard refresh** or purge **full-page cache** if you use one; stale HTML is the other common reason this “still shows.”

If anything remains visible, say what the wrapper class is in DevTools (e.g. `wc-block-breadcrumbs` vs `storefront-breadcrumb` vs SEO plugin breadcrumbs) and we can target that path too.

[REDACTED]

Tracing all breadcrumb/title output paths and applying stronger suppression. Checking WooCommerce's breadcrumb API and broadening PLP detection.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Identifying the issue: Storefront injects the `.storefront-breadcrumb` wrapper via the `woocommerce_breadcrumb_defaults` filter, so breadcrumbs still render whenever anything calls `woocommerce_breadcrumb()` — including the WooCommerce breadcrumbs block. `remove_action` only stops hooked callbacks; blocks and direct calls bypass it. Adding a WooCommerce template override to suppress breadcrumb output on PLPs, restoring `loop/header.php` with a plugin-file fallback, adding `pre_render_block`, and injecting last-resort CSS in `wp_head`.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
<?php
/**
 * Product listing: suppress breadcrumb entirely (blocks + hooks still call woocommerce_breadcrumb()).
 *
 * Non–product-listing views delegate to the WooCommerce core template (avoid wc_get_template recursion).
 *
 * @package WooCommerce/Templates
 */

defined('ABSPATH') || exit;

if (
    function_exists('printonet_theme_is_product_listing')
    && printonet_theme_is_product_listing()
) {
    return;
}

$core = WC()->plugin_path() . '/templates/global/breadcrumb.php';
if (is_readable($core)) {
    include $core;
}

<?php
/**
 * Product listing: no woocommerce-products-header wrapper — archive descriptions only.
 *
 * Other product archives fall back to WooCommerce core loop/header.php (avoid wc_get_template recursion).
 *
 * @package WooCommerce/Templates
 */

defined('ABSPATH') || exit;

if (
    function_exists('printonet_theme_is_product_listing')
    && printonet_theme_is_product_listing()
) {
    /**
     * Hook: woocommerce_archive_description.
     *
     * @hooked woocommerce_taxonomy_archive_description - 10
     * @hooked woocommerce_product_archive_description - 10
     */
    do_action('woocommerce_archive_description');

    return;
}

$core = WC()->plugin_path() . '/templates/loop/header.php';
if (is_readable($core)) {
    include $core;
}

content.text:
Here’s what was actually wrong, and what we changed.

### Why it kept showing

1. **`remove_action('woocommerce_shop_loop_header', 'woocommerce_product_taxonomy_archive_header', …)`**  
   That callback is what loads **`woocommerce/loop/header.php`**. After removing it, your overridden **`loop/header.php` never ran**, so you weren’t really controlling the archive header from the theme file anymore.

2. **`remove_action` doesn’t stop every breadcrumb**  
   Anything that still calls **`woocommerce_breadcrumb()`** will print markup—including the **WooCommerce breadcrumbs block**. Storefront also wraps output via **`woocommerce_breadcrumb_defaults`** (`wrap_before` → `.storefront-breadcrumb`), so you still get that row whenever the function runs.

### What we did (hard stop paths)

1. **`woocommerce/global/breadcrumb.php`** (theme override)  
   On product listing URLs it returns **no output**. Otherwise it **`include`s** the core WooCommerce template from the plugin path so there’s **no `wc_get_template` recursion**.

2. **`woocommerce/loop/header.php`** (restored)  
   On product listings it only runs **`woocommerce_archive_description`**. Otherwise it includes the **core** `loop/header.php` from the plugin.

3. **Stopped removing** `woocommerce_product_taxonomy_archive_header`  
   So WooCommerce again loads your **`loop/header.php`** override on archives.

4. **`pre_render_block`** (priority 999, 3 args)  
   Strips **`woocommerce/breadcrumbs`** and **`core/query-title`** (`type: archive`) before render when possible.

5. **`wp_head` inline CSS** (`printonet-plp-hide-chrome`)  
   Last-resort hiding if HTML is cached or something bypasses PHP.

6. **`printonet_theme_is_product_listing()`**  
   Also treats the dedicated **Shop page** via **`is_page( wc_get_page_id('shop') )`** when the main query flags act oddly.

---

Deploy these files, **purge any page cache / CDN**, hard-refresh. If anything **still** appears, it’s almost certainly **not** WooCommerce core (e.g. Yoast/RankMath breadcrumbs or a page builder block). Paste the **exact outer class** of the remaining element from DevTools and we can target that path too.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Verify PHP syntax after edits

content.text:
Reverting the level heuristic: a Shop page title may use `level: 2`; we'll strip any `core/post-title` that isn't inside the WooCommerce product-collection namespace (official product cards set that).

[REDACTED]

### What “purge” meant  
That only refers to **full-page caches** (hosts like Cloudflare, Kinsta page cache, WP Rocket “cache entire page”, etc.). If your edits show up right away, you don’t need to worry about that — sorry for the confusion.

### Why the **page title** could still show  
The Shop screen is often edited as a **normal Page** in the Site Editor. In that setup the big heading is frequently a **`core/post-title`** block (the Shop **page** title), **not** `core/query-title` with `type: "archive"`.  

Our logic only removed **`core/query-title`** when `type === 'archive'`, so the **`post-title`** block could keep printing **“Shop”** even though everything else was wired.

### What we changed just now  

1. **`woocommerce_page_title` filter** — returns an empty string on product listing URLs so anything still calling `woocommerce_page_title()` doesn’t output text.  

2. **Block stripping helper `printonet_theme_should_strip_plp_block()`**  
   - **`core/query-title`** → stripped on all PLPs (any `type`).  
   - **`core/post-title`** → stripped **unless** `__woocommerceNamespace` starts with `woocommerce/product-collection/` (so **product names in the grid** stay).  

3. **No broad CSS** on `main .wp-block-post-title` — that would have hidden **every** product name in the grid.

After a refresh, the standalone **“Shop”** title should go away while product titles in the loop stay.  

If you still see a heading, open DevTools and note whether it’s **`h1.wp-block-post-title`**, **`core/heading`**, or something else (e.g. Rank Math) — then we can target that exact block/markup.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Identifying the issue: `loop/header.php` falls back to WooCommerce core when `printonet_theme_is_product_listing()` is false (e.g. edge cases with conditionals or template cache), which restores the full `<header class="woocommerce-products-header">`. This template only loads for product archive headers — we'll make it always output archive descriptions only (no fallback). Adding a layout-specific CSS backup targeting `main#main`.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
<?php
/**
 * Product archive header (Shop, categories, attributes): omit WooCommerce default
 * {@see woocommerce-products-header} wrapper and archive title — descriptions only.
 *
 * Loaded exclusively via {@see woocommerce_product_taxonomy_archive_header} on
 * {@see woocommerce_shop_loop_header}; must never delegate back to core (that would
 * resurrect the “Shop” block users delete in previews).
 *
 * @package WooCommerce\Templates
 * @version 8.6.0
 */

defined('ABSPATH') || exit;

/**
 * Hook: woocommerce_archive_description.
 *
 * @hooked woocommerce_taxonomy_archive_description - 10
 * @hooked woocommerce_product_archive_description - 10
 */
do_action('woocommerce_archive_description');

<?php
/**
 * Hide breadcrumbs on product listing URLs only (single product etc. keep them).
 *
 * @package WooCommerce/Templates
 */

defined('ABSPATH') || exit;

$printonet_hide_breadcrumbs = static function (): bool {
    if (!function_exists('is_shop')) {
        return false;
    }

    return is_shop() || is_product_taxonomy()
        || (function_exists('is_post_type_archive') && is_post_type_archive('product'));
};

if ($printonet_hide_breadcrumbs()) {
    return;
}

$core = WC()->plugin_path() . '/templates/global/breadcrumb.php';
if (is_readable($core)) {
    include $core;
}

/**
 * Hide breadcrumbs on product listing URLs only (single product etc. keep them).
 *
 * @package WooCommerce/Templates
 */

defined('ABSPATH') || exit;

$hide = false;
if (function_exists('is_shop') && (is_shop() || is_product_taxonomy())) {
    $hide = true;
} elseif (function_exists('is_post_type_archive') && is_post_type_archive('product')) {
    $hide = true;
}

if ($hide) {
    return;
}

$core = WC()->plugin_path() . '/templates/global/breadcrumb.php';
if (is_readable($core)) {
    include $core;
}

input.description: PHP lint WooCommerce template overrides

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong, and what we changed.

### Cause
Your screenshot is the **classic** WooCommerce layout:  
`main#main.site-main` → **`header.woocommerce-products-header`** → “Shop”.

In [`woocommerce/loop/header.php`](printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/woocommerce/loop/header.php) we used to do:

- If “product listing” → only archive description  
- **Else → `include` WooCommerce core `loop/header.php`**

So whenever that “product listing” check didn’t match (edge cases, stale logic, etc.), WordPress loaded **core** again and brought back the full `<header class="woocommerce-products-header">` with the **Shop** `<h1>`.

That matches what you’re still seeing.

### Fix
1. **`woocommerce/loop/header.php`**  
   It **only** runs `do_action('woocommerce_archive_description')` now.  
   There is **no** fallback to core for this file, so the default “Shop” header block **cannot** come back through this path.

2. **`woocommerce/global/breadcrumb.php`**  
   Same idea: hide breadcrumbs on shop / product archives using simple `is_shop()` / taxonomy / product archive checks (no dependency on the theme helper).

3. **`style.css`**  
   Extra rule:  
   `main#main.site-main > header.woocommerce-products-header { display: none !important; }`  
   so if anything still prints that header, it’s hidden even when body classes aren’t what we expect.

4. **`functions.php`**  
   On **`after_switch_theme`**, call **`wc_clear_template_cache()`** so WooCommerce doesn’t keep an old cached path to templates after updates.

### One-time step if it still looks cached
WooCommerce caches which template file path to use. After pulling these changes, trigger a refresh once:

- Re-save **Appearance → Themes** (switch parent → child and back), **or**
- From WP CLI: `wp eval 'wc_clear_template_cache();'`  
  (or any equivalent that runs `wc_clear_template_cache()` once).

After that, reload the Shop page: the centered **Shop** block should be gone; sorting + grid stay. If anything remains, say whether the markup is still **`header.woocommerce-products-header`** or a different tag/class and we can chase that path next.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
I dont have credentials for this store
</user_query>

We can't access WP Admin or WP CLI without credentials. Adding automatic WooCommerce template-cache busting when theme files change so a deploy alone clears stale cached paths — no login required.

[REDACTED]

You **don’t need store credentials** for these fixes to apply.

### What actually requires “credentials”
- **WP Admin** (Themes, WooCommerce settings, plugins, Clear Cache buttons)
- **WP‑CLI** on the server (`wp …`)

Everything we changed lives in the **child theme files**. Whoever deploys code (Git, CI, copying files onto the server) can ship it **without** a WordPress login—as long as the live site is already running this theme.

### WooCommerce template cache (no login)
WooCommerce can cache **which file path** it uses for overrides. I added logic on **`wp_loaded`** that compares a fingerprint of:

- `woocommerce/loop/header.php`
- `woocommerce/global/breadcrumb.php`

When those files change (new deploy / new `mtime`), it runs **`wc_clear_template_cache()`** once automatically. The **first normal page hit** after deploy does that—still **no wp-admin**.

### If you have neither WP nor server access
Then someone who **does** control hosting or the repo has to deploy the theme. Your role can stay “author the patch in Git”; you’re not blocked by missing merchant credentials.

### If you only lack WP but have SSH
You still wouldn’t need WP: deploy files + let the site load once (auto flush), or touch nothing extra if deploy already updated those PHP files.

So: **missing store login doesn’t stop this approach**; it only stops you from clicking around inside `wp-admin`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
I dont have a way to clear the cache
</user_query>

Confirming WooCommerce template cache auto-clears when our theme files change. Expanding the fingerprint so deploys that only touch `functions.php` also trigger a flush, and documenting limits of full-page/CDN caches (those require the host or TTL unless PHP runs).

[REDACTED]

You don’t need a “clear cache” button for the important part.

### WooCommerce template cache (inside WordPress)
The theme already runs **`wc_clear_template_cache()`** automatically when these files change (their **`mtime`** on disk):

- `functions.php`
- `style.css`
- `woocommerce/loop/header.php`
- `woocommerce/global/breadcrumb.php`

That happens on the **next request where PHP runs** (usually right after deploy). No wp-admin, no WP‑CLI, no manual purge.

I’ve **included `functions.php` and `style.css`** in that fingerprint so a typical deploy triggers the flush even if only those files changed.

### Browser cache for CSS
Your theme already sets the stylesheet version from **`filemtime(style.css)`**, so when `style.css` changes, browsers normally fetch it again without you doing anything.

### Full-page / CDN cache (Cloudflare, Varnish, host “page cache”)
Those layers sometimes serve **saved HTML without hitting PHP**. In that situation:

- WordPress **cannot** clear them by itself  
- They expire after **TTL**, or someone with hosting/CDN access runs a purge  

Workarounds that sometimes bypass edge caches without credentials:

- Open the shop URL with a **fresh query string** (only helps if the CDN treats URLs with different query params as different caches), e.g. `…/shop/?_=1735689600`  
- Use **private/incognito** to rule out your **browser** cache  

So: **you’re not blocked by lacking a cache UI for WooCommerce’s template cache**—that’s handled when deploy updates files. If something still looks old after deploy, it’s likely **edge/HTML caching**, which needs TTL expiry or whoever controls the CDN/host—not store WP credentials.

[REDACTED]

Implementing fixes so changes apply reliably: WooCommerce may be serving cached template paths or stale fingerprints (mtimes preserved on deploy). Forcing child-theme template resolution and switching the fingerprint to content hashes (`md5_file`).

input.description: Find woocommerce_locate_template filter in WooCommerce
Read wc_locate_template signature and parameters
Read wc_clear_template_cache implementation
Read wc_get_template() for cache vs locate_template order

content.text:
Identifying the issue: WooCommerce caches resolved template paths in `wc_get_template()`. On cache hits it skips `wc_locate_template()`, so `woocommerce_locate_template` never runs and stale paths persist. Fixing by filtering `wc_get_template` (runs every load, including cache hits) to force child-theme overrides, plus content-based fingerprints.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Verify PHP syntax after wc_get_template filter

content.text:
Here’s why it could look like **nothing changed**, even though the files are correct.

### What was wrong
WooCommerce **`wc_get_template()` caches the resolved file path** in the object cache. On a **cache hit**, it **does not** call `wc_locate_template()` again, so:

- New files under `yourtheme/woocommerce/…` may never get picked up until that cache is cleared, and  
- Filters on **`woocommerce_locate_template`** don’t help on those hits.

Deploys that **keep the same file mtimes** can also stop our fingerprint-based **`wc_clear_template_cache()`** from ever firing.

### What we changed

1. **`wc_get_template` filter (priority 999)**  
   For **`loop/header.php`** and **`global/breadcrumb.php`**, every load **rewrites the path** to the child theme file **when it exists**. That runs **even when WooCommerce has a cached path**, so overrides apply without you clearing anything.

2. **Fingerprint based on `md5_file()`**  
   We hash the **contents** of `functions.php`, `style.css`, and the two WooCommerce overrides so **deploys that preserve mtimes** still trigger **`wc_clear_template_cache()`** when the files actually change.

3. **Stronger inline CSS guard**  
   The `wp_head` snippet now also matches **`main#main.site-main > header.woocommerce-products-header`** and uses a **fallback PLP check** if the helper ever disagrees with `is_shop()` / taxonomy / product archive.

After this ships to the server, **one normal page view** (PHP must run—not only a CDN HTML snapshot) should pick up the child templates.

### If it still looks unchanged
Then the HTML is probably **not coming from this PHP/theme at all** (full-page cache, different theme active on that URL, or you’re not deploying to the environment you’re viewing). In DevTools → Network, confirm the response is a **200 from your origin** and View Source contains **`printonet-plp-hide-chrome`** in `<head>`; if that style tag is missing, this theme isn’t what served the page.

[REDACTED]

input.command: git config --global --get user.name 2>/dev/null; git config --global --get user.email 2>/dev/null; git config --local --get user.name 2>/dev/null; git config --local --get user.email 2>/dev/null
input.description: Show global and local git user.name / user.email if set

content.text:
Git is blocking the operation because **no commit author identity** is set. Nothing is wrong with your repo; you just need to tell Git who you are.

### Option A — set it for everything on this machine (usual choice)

```bash
git config --global user.name "Your Real Name"
git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"
```

Use the **same email** you use on GitHub/GitLab if you want commits tied to your account.

### Option B — only for this repository

```bash
cd /path/to/your/repo
git config user.name "Your Real Name"
git config user.email "your.email@example.com"
```

### Check that it worked

```bash
git config --global --list | grep user
```

(or omit `--global` if you used repo-local config.)

Then retry whatever failed (commit, rebase, merge, etc.).

**Note:** Replace the name and email with **your** values; don’t use placeholders in real commits. I didn’t run `git config` for you because I don’t know your real name or email—once you set those locally, the error goes away.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Count PLP style vs products header on live HTML
Live theme Version header in style.css

content.text:
The storefront isn’t ignoring your edits — **those edits aren’t on the server Royal uses yet.**

Here’s proof from the live site right now:

| Check | Live (`royal.stores.printonet.com`) | Your workspace |
|--------|-------------------------------------|----------------|
| `style.css` size | **20 271 bytes** | **23 785 bytes** |
| Theme `Version` in `style.css` | **0.1.0** | **0.1.1** |
| Shop HTML contains `printonet-plp-hide-chrome` | **no** (0 hits) | would appear after deploy |
| Shop HTML still has `woocommerce-products-header` | **yes** | overridden in current code |

So WordPress is still loading an **older copy** of `printonet-theme` than what’s in this repo.

### What actually fixes it

Someone with deploy/host access must **copy the theme folder onto the Printonet WordPress install** (same path the stores use):

- From repo: `printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/`  
- On server: `wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/`  
  (replace with your real `WP_PATH`).

Your multisite installer already does exactly that kind of sync; see:

```70:70:printonet-multitenant/scripts/install_wp_multisite.sh
rsync -a --delete "${REPO_ROOT}/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/" "${WP_PATH}/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/"
```

After a successful sync:

1. Reload [`https://royal.stores.printonet.com/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/style.css`](https://royal.stores.printonet.com/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/style.css) — `Version:` should read **0.1.1** and byte size should match ~23.8k.  
2. View source on [`/shop/`](https://royal.stores.printonet.com/shop/) — you should see `<style id="printonet-plp-hide-chrome">` and the old `# Shop` header block should be gone (or hidden, depending on path).

### If you “deploy” only Git push

Pushing to GitHub does **nothing** to Royal until your pipeline or ops runs **`rsync`/deploy** (or rebuilds images that include `wp-content/themes/printonet-theme`). Confirm that step exists and ran successfully after your commits.

Until that sync happens, **no amount of local/theme coding will change Royal’s shop page.**

[REDACTED]
